title: Control-Intent Predicate
description: An abstracted, human-readable expression of what it means for a control's intent to be satisfied, stated over required-configuration state. This is deliberately a documentation schema, not an executable policy language: it captures the conditions a verifier must establish, without prescribing or revealing any particular verification engine. The conditions are evaluated over the WHOLE relevant configuration, not a single resource or line.

statement.description: Plain-language statement of the control's intent, e.g. 'No path exists by which the bucket is publicly readable.'

evaluation_scope.description: What must be considered to judge satisfaction.

conditions.description: The conjunction of conditions that must ALL hold for intent to be satisfied. Each is a human-readable, checkable assertion over configuration state.

assert.description: A single checkable condition, e.g. 'aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block has all four block flags = true for the target bucket.'

rationale.description: Why this condition is necessary for the control's intent (often the reason a linter that omits it produces a silent failure).

common_bypasses.description: Known ways a change can pass a linter yet fail this predicate. These are the traps the lint-pass-but-intent-unmet cases exercise.
